title: How Seeds Grow
content:
Every great tree starts as a tiny seed. But how does a seed know how to grow? Let's discover the amazing journey of a seed becoming a plant.

A seed is like a tiny package. Inside it has everything needed to grow: a baby plant, called an embryo, and food to help it start growing.

The seed has a hard coat to protect it. It can wait for months or even years until conditions are just right.

When the seed gets enough water, warmth, and air, something magical happens. The seed begins to wake up!

First, the seed soaks up water like a tiny sponge. The water makes the seed swell and softens its coat.

Next, a small root pushes out of the seed and grows down into the soil. This is called germination. The root anchors the plant and searches for water and nutrients.

Then a tiny shoot grows upward, pushing through the soil toward the light. This is the stem.

When the shoot reaches sunlight, leaves begin to unfold. Now the plant can make its own food through photosynthesis.

The leaves absorb sunlight and carbon dioxide from the air. The roots absorb water. The plant mixes these together to make sugar for energy and oxygen for us to breathe.

The plant grows bigger and bigger. Eventually, it makes flowers. The flowers turn into fruits with seeds inside.

Those seeds fall to the ground, and the cycle begins again.

From a tiny seed to a mighty tree—that's the wonder of nature!

The End.
